agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to replace 1965 zoning bylaw with new 2026 version
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on repealing Burnaby's current Zoning Bylaw (from 1965) and replacing it with a new Zoning Bylaw 2026. This proposed change would also introduce new Development Permit Area Guidelines and reassign some properties to different zoning districts. If approved, the new bylaw would update rules for what can be built and where, affecting many local businesses and property owners.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on how the new zoning rules could affect your property or business.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
BURNABY ZONING BYLAW - PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT
Purpose: To seek Council approval to repeal Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Housing Authority Year 2 operating funding request
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation's request for Year 2 operating funding. This is a proposal to provide ongoing financial support to the housing authority for its second year of operations. The decision has not been made yet — council will vote on whether to approve the funding at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2
BHA - ASSISTANCE REQUEST FOR YEAR 2 OPERATING FUNDING
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s assistance request for Year 2 Operating Funding.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 2026 property tax rates for Burnaby businesses
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on proposed 2026 property tax rates for municipal, regional, and Business Improvement Area (BIA) properties. If approved, these rates will determine how much property tax businesses and other property owners in Burnaby will pay in 2026. The decision is not final yet — Council will consider and vote on the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council meeting date and attend or submit comments if you want to have a say on the proposed tax rates.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3
2026 PROPERTY TAX RATES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for 2026 municipal, regional and business improvement area (BIA) property tax rates.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to appoint an Approving Officer
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to appoint an Approving Officer. An Approving Officer is a person who has the authority to approve plans for new subdivisions (dividing land into smaller lots) and road closures. This is a routine administrative appointment, but it affects anyone who needs to subdivide land or close a road in Burnaby. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider and vote on the appointment at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from you at this time. Watch for the council decision if you plan to subdivide land or close a road in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4
APPOINTMENT OF APPROVING OFFICER
Purpose: To seek Council approval to appoint an Approving Officer.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on retaining wall height variance at 8012 11th Ave
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a Development Variance Permit (DVP) for a property at 8012 11th Avenue. A DVP is a special permission that lets a property owner break certain rules in the city's zoning bylaw. In this case, the owner wants permission to build retaining walls that are taller than what the current rules allow. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider and vote on this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you own or are developing property near 8012 11th Avenue, watch for the council decision — it could set a precedent for similar height variance requests in your area.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5
DVP #26-01 - 8012 11TH AVENUE - RETAINING WALLS HEIGHT VARIANCES
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Development Variance Permit (DVP) #26-01.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to award insurance contracts for property and liability programs
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on awarding new contracts for the city's annual property and liability insurance programs. These contracts cover insurance for city-owned buildings, equipment, and general liability risks. A decision is expected at the upcoming council meeting. If approved, the new insurance contracts would take effect for the 2026 policy year. This does not directly change any rules for businesses, but it affects how the city manages its own insurance coverage.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.6
2026 INSURANCE PROGRAMS - CONTRACT RENEWALS
Purpose: To request approval to award insurance contracts for the annual property and liability insurance programs.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to approve Heritage Commission members attending 2026 BC conference
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to allow members of Burnaby's Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC, from May 4-6, 2026. The CHC advises Council on heritage matters, and this trip would help members learn about best practices and network with other heritage professionals. The decision is not yet final — Council will consider the request at an upcoming meeting.

key_dates.label: Heritage BC Conference in Squamish takes place

agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.7
2026 HERITAGE BC CONFERENCE
Purpose: To seek Council approval for members of the Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC from May 4-6, 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract award for networking software licenses
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses. If approved, this would authorize the city to purchase the software needed to run its computer networks. This is a routine procurement decision that keeps city technology systems working, but it does not directly create new rules or fees for businesses.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.8
CA - NETWORKING SOFTWARE LICENSES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for new civic facility furniture
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply, delivery, and installation of office and facility furniture for a new civic facility. If approved, this contract would cover all the furniture and equipment needed to fit out the new building. The decision is on the agenda for the upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.9
CA - NEW CIVIC FACILITY FIT-OUT, FURNISHINGS AND EQUIPMENT
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply, delivery and installation of office and facility furniture.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract change for external audit services
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change for the city's external audit services. This means council will decide whether to approve a change to the agreement with the company that audits the city's financial records. If approved, the change would alter the terms of the existing contract. The decision is not final yet — council will discuss and vote on it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.10
CC - EXTERNAL AUDIT SERVICES
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for External Audit Services.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for geotechnical work at Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (a formal amendment to an existing agreement) for geotechnical consulting services at the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex. Geotechnical consultants study soil and ground conditions to ensure buildings are safe and stable. If approved, this change would adjust the scope or cost of the consultant's work on the project. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider and vote on it at the upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from you at this time. Watch for the council decision if your business is involved in the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.11
CC - BURNABY LAKE RECREATION COMPLEX GEOTECHNICAL CONSULTING SERVICES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for geotechnical consultant services for the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on Spring 2026 community grant recipients
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving the recommended recipients for the Spring 2026 community grant intake. This decision will determine which local organizations and projects receive funding from the city. The grants are part of the city's regular program to support community groups, non-profits, and events that benefit Burnaby residents. If approved, the selected applicants will receive their grant money to carry out their proposed activities. The council meeting agenda lists this item for discussion and a final vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
ECC - SPRING 2026 COMMUNITY GRANT APPLICATIONS
Purpose: To seek Council approval for the recommended grant recipients for the Spring 2026 intake.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Mid-year 2026 fee changes to Burnaby Fees Bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on mid-year 2026 amendments to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This proposed bylaw would change certain fees that businesses and residents pay for city services, permits, or licences. The exact fees affected are not listed in this agenda item, but the decision could impact what you pay for various city services later in 2026. Watch for the full list of fee changes before the vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the full list of proposed fee changes before the council vote — this could affect your business costs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW - 2026 MID-YEAR AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To propose mid-year 2026 fee amendments to certain fee schedules within the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed Tenant Protection Bylaw and framework. If approved, this new bylaw (a local law) would create new rules to protect renters in the city. The proposal includes a framework that outlines how the bylaw would work. Council will decide whether to endorse the plan at an upcoming meeting. This is not yet law — it is a proposal under consideration.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, the bylaw could affect how you manage rental properties or your rights as a tenant.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.3
PDC - PROPOSED TENANT PROTECTION FRAMEWORK AND TENANT PROTECTION BYLAW AND ASSOCIATED BYLAW AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement of the proposed tenant protection framework and Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw and associated bylaw amendments.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on Southeast Bikeway upgrades
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on proposed improvements for the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project. If approved, this project would add new bike lanes, crossings, and safety features along the route. The decision is not final yet — Council will discuss and vote on the plan at an upcoming meeting. Business owners near the bikeway should watch for the outcome, as construction could affect local traffic and parking.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, prepare for possible construction impacts on nearby streets and parking.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.4
TC - SOUTHEAST BIKEWAY UPGRADE PROJECT - PROPOSED IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the proposed improvements as part of the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update design standards for safer intersections
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to update the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards. The change would require new street designs to include 'protected intersection geometry' — a safer layout that separates pedestrians and cyclists from turning vehicles. If approved, this would apply to new developments and road projects in the Town Centre area. The goal is to make walking and biking safer for people of all ages and abilities.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this will affect how you design new developments or road projects in Burnaby's Town Centre.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.5
TC - PROPOSED AMENDMENT TO THE BURNABY TOWN CENTRE DESIGN STANDARDS TO INCORPORATE PROTECTED INTERSECTION GEOMETRY
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement to amend the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards to incorporate protected intersection geometry, improve road safety for pedestrians and cyclists and support all-ages-and-abilities mobility.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby staff recommend against exempting Brentwood Park from SSMUH rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a staff report recommending against exempting Brentwood Park properties from provincial Small-Scale Multi-Unit Housing (SSMUH) requirements. Staff found that restrictive covenants and statutory building schemes are private property interests the City is not party to, and that the City is bound by provincial law to zone for SSMUH. A proposed Building Bylaw amendment to require compliance with such covenants would need to apply city-wide to all property types, including residential, commercial, and industrial, and would add application costs and process time.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for council's decision on the staff recommendation at the April 28, 2026 meeting.

key_dates.label: Council receives staff report on Brentwood Park SSMUH exemption

agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.1
BRENTWOOD PARK SSMUH MOTION - RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS AND STATUTORY BUILDING SCHEMES
Purpose: To respond to Council’s September 9, 2025, motion regarding exempting Brentwood Park properties with Restrictive Covenants from Provincial SSMUH requirements.

attachments.title: Brentwood Park SSMUH Motion - Restrictive Covenants and Statutory Building Schem

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive building permit activity report for March 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a report on building permits issued in Burnaby from March 1 to March 31, 2026. This is an information-only item — no vote or decision is required. The report gives a snapshot of construction activity in the city, including the number and types of permits issued. It does not propose any new rules or changes.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2
SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 03 FROM MARCH 1 - MARCH 31, 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Heritage designation for 3789 Royal Oak Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a report and Statement of Significance for the heritage resources at 3789 Royal Oak Avenue. This is the first step toward possibly designating the property as a municipal heritage site. If approved, future renovations or demolition would require special permits and review. The decision has not been made yet — Council is gathering information first.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed now. Watch for future council meetings where a formal heritage designation bylaw may be proposed for a vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.3
CHC - AS-FOUND REPORT AND STATEMENT OF SIGNIFICANCE FOR HERITAGE RESOURCES AT 3789 ROYAL OAK AVENUE
Purpose: To provide Council an as-found report and Statement of Significance for heritage resources located at 7389 Royal Oak Avenue for information.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby solid waste and recycling 2025 annual report
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider the City of Burnaby's Solid Waste and Recycling 2025 Annual Report. This report summarizes how the city's garbage, recycling, and organics collection system performed last year. It is a proposed update for council to review — no new rules or fees have been decided yet. The report may lead to future changes in collection services or policies.
agenda_briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.4
EC - SOLID WASTE AND RECYCLING (SWR) 2025 ANNUAL REPORT
Purpose: To provide Council with a summary of the City of Burnaby solid waste and recycling collection system performance.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive update on proposed food sustainability measures in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive an operational update on proposed food sustainability measures. This update will inform Council about current programs and potential future actions to improve food security and reduce waste in the city. No final decisions have been made yet, but this update could lead to new policies or programs that affect local food businesses.
agenda_briefs.industries: Food Service, Grocery & Food Retail, Agriculture
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.5
EC - SU: ENHANCING FOOD SUSTAINABILITY IN BURNABY
Purpose: To provide Council with an operational update on food sustainability measures within the City of Burnaby.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Safety upgrades at Central Boulevard and Metropolis parkade intersection
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and the Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access. The proposal includes changes to make the area safer for drivers, pedestrians, and cyclists. A decision has not been made yet — Council will review the plan at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, construction could affect traffic and access near your business.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.6
TC - CENTRAL BOULEVARD AND PARKADE INERSECTION SAFETY IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of the proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezone four Burnaby properties for taller towers and a hotel
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw amendment that would change the zoning for four properties near the Carrigan site (3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Rd, 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place). The developer wants to replace some planned office space with a hotel and restaurants, add 362 more market strata (condo) units, and make two towers taller — Tower 1B would go from 80 to 87 storeys, and Tower 1A from 73 to 77 storeys. The proposal also changes how the developer meets its requirement to include affordable housing, by providing it only for Phase 1 and moving that obligation to the Carrigan site instead of on these properties. This is a change from the previous rezoning that was already approved in September 2024.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit a written comment to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14808 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 7, 2026 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Rd., 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place)
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73 (Tower 1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. As part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to replace a portion of the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses, and add 362 additional market strata units from the development concept approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional seven storeys for Tower 1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In addition, as part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to provide the inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from the previous rezoning approval.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 12-storey mixed-use tower at 6535 Kingsway, Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw change that would rezone 6535 Kingsway. The plan is to allow a 12-storey building with 290 homes, shops on the ground floor, and underground parking. This is a proposal only — it has not been approved yet. The change would update the city's zoning rules (the official list of what can be built where) to match the city's long-term growth plan.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit a written comment to have your say before Council votes.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14809 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 8, 2026 - REZ #25-09 (6535 Kingsway)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use multiple-family development with a double-height commercial storey at street level comprised of 290 residential units, ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the applicable OCP and the Edmonds Community Plan designations for the site.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezone Norland Avenue for five-storey self-storage building
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw to rezone 2757 Norland Avenue to allow construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The proposal follows the 1998 Official Community Plan policies and approved transition rules. A final decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14810 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 9, 2026 - REZ #25-21 (2757 Norland Avenue)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The proposal is being advanced in accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies and approved transitionary provisions.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot bylaw amendment
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw that would create a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, this would change the rules for where and how these devices can be used on city streets. The decision has not been made yet — this is an upcoming proposal on the council agenda.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this pilot could affect how your business operates if you use or sell e-bikes or e-scooters.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot in parks
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would allow e-bikes and electric kick scooters in certain Burnaby parks as part of a pilot program. The amendment to the Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 would create new rules for where and how these devices can be used. This is not yet law — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this pilot could affect where you can ride e-bikes and scooters in Burnaby parks.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment for e-bike and e-scooter pilot enforcement
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to support a new pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, this change would allow the city to issue tickets and enforce rules specifically for these devices during the pilot. The amendment is needed to make sure the city can properly manage and regulate the pilot program.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect how e-bikes and e-scooters are regulated in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026 (e-bike and electric kick scooter programs)
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2026 property tax rates bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would set the 2026 tax rates for all land and buildings (called 'improvements') in Burnaby. This bylaw is required under sections 197 and 216 of BC's Community Charter, which gives cities the power to set property tax rates each year. If approved, this bylaw will determine how much property tax you pay in 2026 based on your property's assessed value.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter
